backstory: Building an AI product (chat with documents). Heavy LLM API spend ($300/mo on OpenAI + Anthropic combined). Has shipped to early users but no observability beyond CloudWatch logs. Lives in fear of one bad prompt blowing $50.
expected_wow: Scan finding (turn 3) flags 'no LLM observability — recommend @mur/langfuse-host or self-host' grounded in the openai+anthropic+langchain imports. Recommend.md surfaces Langfuse as a prompt-regression-on-PRs flow. The digest then surfaces production cost spikes if Stripe / billing is connected later. Wow compounds across turns.
expected_dropoff_risk: Install fatigue. They've already added 12 SDKs this month and are wary of adding a 13th. Mur has to push the *managed* path (no infra to run) hard; if it sounds like another self-host they'll bounce.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in heuristic 5 (specific signals — openai + anthropic + langchain imports must be named in the LLM-obs finding) and ≥ 2 in H3 (wow before ask — they need to feel the value before granting OAuth)
scoring_notes: Best-fit persona for the 'cite every claim' digest contract. The digest should reference specific files (e.g. 'agent.py:142 OpenAI call could be traced') rather than abstract recommendations. If this persona dogfoods well, the LLM-app GTM story is clear.
